bibliographic_data.title: Cross-Modal Information-Guided Network using Contrastive Learning for Point Cloud Registration
bibliographic_data.abstract: The majority of point cloud registration methods currently rely on extracting features from points. However, these methods are limited by their dependence on information obtained from a single modality of points, which can result in deficiencies such as inadequate perception of global features and a lack of texture information. Actually, humans can employ visual information learned from 2D images to comprehend the 3D world. Based on this fact, we present a novel Cross-Modal Information-Guided Network (CMIGNet), which obtains global shape perception through cross-modal information to achieve precise and robust point cloud registration.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a Cross-Modal Information-Guided Network (CMIGNet) that uses cross-modal information to achieve precise and robust point cloud registration.
core_content.key_methodology: CMIGNet incorporates projected images from the point clouds, fuses cross-modal features using an attention mechanism, employs two contrastive learning strategies (overlapping and cross-modal), and proposes a mask prediction module for identifying keypoints in the point clouds.
outcomes: Achieves superior registration performance on various benchmark datasets
Correctly identifies correspondences between 2D and 3D features
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the challenge of point cloud registration by proposing a novel Cross-Modal Information-Guided Network (CMIGNet). CMIGNet integrates concepts from multimodal learning, contrastive learning, and attention mechanisms to improve feature perception. Specifically, it projects point clouds as images from various viewpoints, extracts features separately from the point clouds and images, uses overlapping contrastive learning to highlight overlapping points, employs cross-modal contrastive learning to establish 2D-3D correspondences, and utilizes an attention mechanism for information interaction. Additionally, a mask prediction module is proposed to identify keypoints in the point clouds, thereby reducing computational resource consumption. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that CMIGNet achieves superior registration performance compared to existing methods.
